specialist_responses.database: Payment-db shows sudden full table scans on transactions. The transaction_id index appears dropped in the last migration; query volume is normal but latency is huge.
specialist_responses.network: No packet loss or elevated retransmits. North-south traffic looks normal; not a network issue.
specialist_responses.security: No DDoS indicators. WAF and edge metrics are normal; no anomalous auth or bot spikes.
specialist_responses.oncall: Recent deployment on payment-api included a schema migration touching transactions. Rollback is a safe first move while we validate DB indexes.

ground_truth_root_cause: Dropped transaction_id index on payment-db during recent deployment causing full table scans and elevated latency/5xx.

incident_alert.title: API gateway intermittent NXDOMAIN to internal services

specialist_responses.database: DB metrics are stable. No connection spikes or query anomalies related to this incident.
specialist_responses.network: DNS resolver is returning NXDOMAIN for *.internal. Looks like a config issue after infra change; restarting resolver or rolling back config should help.
specialist_responses.security: No indicators of malicious traffic. DNS failures appear internal and correlate with config change.
specialist_responses.oncall: Gateway errors coincide with CoreDNS config rollout. Recommend restarting dns-resolver and validating forward zones.

ground_truth_root_cause: DNS resolver misconfiguration after infra change causing intermittent internal name resolution failures.

incident_alert.title: Checkout latency and auth failures due to cache outage

specialist_responses.database: DB connections spiked due to cache bypass. DB is stressed but secondary; primary issue seems cache availability/memory.
specialist_responses.network: No infra network issues. Service-to-service connectivity is fine; failures are application-layer.
specialist_responses.security: No DDoS indicators. Symptoms match an internal dependency outage.
specialist_responses.oncall: Redis is OOM and refusing writes. Restarting redis-cluster and adjusting eviction policy/memory limit would mitigate quickly.

ground_truth_root_cause: Redis out-of-memory leading to cache outage and cascading load onto DB and dependent services.
red_herrings: brief network jitter reported by one host

incident_alert.title: Order service memory leak after deployment

specialist_responses.database: DB is normal. Latency is coming from app GC/heap pressure; not DB-bound.
specialist_responses.network: Network is stable. No packet loss or routing changes.
specialist_responses.security: No indicators of compromise or traffic anomalies.
specialist_responses.oncall: Heap usage started climbing immediately after v2.4.1 deploy. Rollback to v2.4.0 should restore health.

ground_truth_root_cause: Memory leak introduced in order-service v2.4.1 causing GC pressure and increasing latency.
red_herrings: slight db cpu increase from retries

incident_alert.title: Customer complaints: incorrect order totals, systems look healthy

specialist_responses.database: DB metrics are normal. If totals are wrong, it’s likely application logic or numeric precision, not DB.
specialist_responses.network: No connectivity issues. Latency and packet loss are nominal.
specialist_responses.security: No signs of fraud or credential abuse. Complaints are consistent with a calculation bug.
specialist_responses.oncall: The v3.1.0 pricing deploy changed numeric types for performance. Suggest rollback and add data integrity checks.

ground_truth_root_cause: Floating point precision regression in pricing-service v3.1.0 causing silent rounding errors for high-priced items.
